articles.title: Oil Market Completely Broken
articles.description:
Oil climbed after renewed clashes between US and Iranian forces which has jeopardized the outlook for a deal to end the war. The Strait of Hormuz has been effectively closed since the start of the war triggering an energy supply shock. Vandana Hari, Founder of VANDA Insights told Bloomberg’s Horizons Middle East and Africa anchor Joumanna Bercetche, oil markets are not reflecting reality.
 (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: ISI-linked module busted: Delhi temple, military camp, UP police stations were on target list
articles.description: Pakistan's ISI allegedly plotted terror attacks in Delhi and Haryana, targeting a historic temple, a popular highway dhaba, and a military camp. Operatives linked to the Shahzad Bhatti module conducted reconnaissance, sharing photos and videos with handlers. The plan aimed to cause panic, disruption, and mass casualties, with police stations in Uttar Pradesh also on the radar.

articles.title: Scientists find natural compounds that hit COVID-19 from every angle
articles.description: A little-known tree from Brazil’s Atlantic Forest may hold a surprising weapon against COVID-19. Researchers discovered that compounds called galloylquinic acids, extracted from its leaves, can attack SARS-CoV-2 on multiple fronts—blocking the virus from entering cells, disrupting its replication, and even dampening harmful inflammation. Unlike many antivirals that target just one part of the virus, these natural compounds act in several ways at once, potentially making it harder for resistance to develop.

articles.title: Koruvada farmers in Anakapalli seek correction of land records after Jagananna resurvey
articles.description: Eight farmers say their lands in Survey No. 327/4 were wrongly recorded as inam lands despite holding pattas and cultivation records.

articles.title: NCRB report: Crime against elders spikes, Bengaluru has second highest crimes against women
articles.description: Bengaluru’s crime rate stood at 117 cases per lakh population, significantly higher than Mumbai’s 74.6 and Chennai’s 25.4.

articles.title: Dead foetus kept in womb ‘for 22 hours’ in Maharashtra’s Beed; health dept. forms committee to probe
articles.description: Family of the woman has alleged that the doctor attempted to hide the negligence and gain financial benefits

articles.title: Your US passport can be revoked over child support debt — Thousands affected under new crackdown
articles.description: The US State Department will revoke passports of parents owing over USD 100,000 in child support, impacting about 2,700 individuals.

articles.title: Why Iran War Risks Hit Indian Stocks Harder
articles.description: Record foreign outflows and surging energy costs are battering India’s economy and the rupee. While geopolitical tensions drive crude prices higher, some sectors show resilience amid the volatility. Lakshmi Iyer of Bajaj Finserv joined Haslinda Amin on Insight to discuss why market corrections offer opportunities and where she sees value in alternatives. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Why Stocks and Bonds Are Responding Differently to the Iran War
articles.description: Stock investors are betting that companies will make enormous profits, despite the war. But investors in bonds, including U.S. Treasuries, have other concerns.

articles.title: ISI-linked module busted: Delhi temple, military camp, UP police stations were on target list
articles.description: Pakistan's ISI allegedly plotted terror attacks in Delhi and Haryana, targeting a historic temple, a popular highway dhaba, and a military camp. Operatives linked to the Shahzad Bhatti module conducted reconnaissance, sharing photos and videos with handlers. The plan aimed to cause panic, disruption, and mass casualties, with police stations in Uttar Pradesh also on the radar.

articles.title: Pakistan's ISI-linked module planned attacks on Delhi temple, dhaba, military camp: Report
articles.description: Pakistan's ISI planned attacks on a historic Delhi temple, a dhaba on the Delhi-Sonipat highway, and a Haryana military camp through operatives linked to the Shahzad Bhatti module, as reported by PTI.

articles.title: Oil Market Completely Broken
articles.description:
Oil climbed after renewed clashes between US and Iranian forces which has jeopardized the outlook for a deal to end the war. The Strait of Hormuz has been effectively closed since the start of the war triggering an energy supply shock. Vandana Hari, Founder of VANDA Insights told Bloomberg’s Horizons Middle East and Africa anchor Joumanna Bercetche, oil markets are not reflecting reality.
 (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: West Bengal government formation LIVE: BJP legislature party meet to elect new West Bengal CM
articles.description: The swearing-in ceremony of the State's first BJP government is scheduled to be held at Brigade Parade Ground on May 9

topStories.title: ‘Not a Chance Hormuz Opens’: How Wall Street’s new NACHO trade bets on a prolonged oil shock
topStories.description: Traders are embracing the “NACHO” trade, betting Hormuz disruptions and elevated oil prices will persist longer than expected.

topStories.title: Hantavirus live updates: CDC classifies outbreak as 'Level 3' emergency response
topStories.description: The MV Hondius cruise ship, on which the hantavirus infection was identified, is making its way to the Canary Islands.

topStories.title: No evidence Austin mass shooter suspect was connected to terrorist organization: FBI
topStories.description: No evidence Austin shooter was "associated with a Foreign Terrorist Organization," FBI concludes

topStories.title: WATCH:  Watch the sweet moment an Army son surprises mom at her college graduation
topStories.description: Blane Yuhas surprised his mom Sabrina Hill at her graduation after nine months apart. This Mother's Day, let our sponsor Macy's be your guide to gifting.

topStories.title: Lakers fall 2-0 down to Thunder as Pistons also double lead
topStories.description: Chet Holmgren and Shai Gilgeous-Alexander each scored 22 points as the Oklahoma City Thunder beat the Los Angeles Lakers 125-107 on Thursday night to take a 2-0 lead in the Western Conference semi-final series.

topStories.title: AI boom masks rising global energy risks, warns David Roche
topStories.description: Market veterans warn investors may be underestimating the long-term economic fallout from the Middle East energy crisis. While AI spending and US economic resilience fuel optimism, falling oil reserves and tightening supplies could lead to shortages and a significant global GDP contraction.  Rising shipping costs and insurance premiums further exacerbate these concerns.

topStories.title: Toyota fourth-quarter profit misses by wide margin as U.S. tariffs drive 49% slump
topStories.description: The world's largest automaker by sales volume saw a 1.89% year-on-year rise in revenue during the fourth quarter ended March.

topStories.title: The Canvas Hack Is a New Kind of Ransomware Debacle
topStories.description: Thousands of schools around the US were paralyzed on Thursday after education tech firm Instructure shut down access to its Canvas platform following a breach by hackers going by the name ShinyHunters.

topStories.title: China Sees a ‘Giant With a Limp’ as U.S. Drains Weapons on Iran War
topStories.description: America’s ability to deter China in a war over Taiwan is weakened, Chinese analysts say, giving Beijing leverage in an upcoming summit with President Trump.

topStories.title: Congress Leader Criticises "Puppet" Governor Over Vijay Majority Row
topStories.description: Superstar actor Vijay's Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam swept 108 of 234 seats - on its electoral debut - in last month's Assembly election to finish as the single largest party.

dailyNews.description: Already having faced an energy crisis five years ago, more European consumers believe they are “one Trump-ignited war away” from crushing costs.

dailyNews.description: As President Trump again voiced optimism that Iran will "make a deal" to end the war, Tehran declared itself the regulator of Strait of Hormuz shipping.

dailyNews.title: Learn more about "48 Hours"
dailyNews.description: True crime. Real justice. The one to watch on Saturday night.

dailyNews.title: Oil prices jump as US, Iran trade fire in Strait of Hormuz
dailyNews.description: Brent crude rises amid clashes in critical waterway.

dailyNews.title: Accused WHCD shooter seeks to disqualify Pirro, Blanche because they attended event
dailyNews.description: Attorneys for accused White House Correspondents' Dinner shooter Cole Allen asked a judge to disqualify Jeanine Pirro and other senior Justice Department leaders from the case because they were present during the incident.

dailyNews.title: Trump praises Susie Wiles’ cancer fight in surprise gala video: ‘Winning it decisively’
dailyNews.description: White House Chief of Staff Susie Wiles disclosed her cancer diagnosis at a gala where Trump praised her courage, toughness and decisive recovery.

dailyNews.title: Another LIV golfer remains committed to staying put: 'I have full faith in the future of LIV'
dailyNews.description: Thomas Detry says he's fully committed to LIV Golf and calls the product amazing, urging all players to show cohesion amid funding uncertainty.

dailyNews.title: Mail-a-handgun: Trump administration pushes to allow firearms to be delivered by USPS
dailyNews.description: The Trump administration is pushing the Unites States Postal Service to allow people to send concealable handguns in the mail after the Department of Justice declared a 99-year-old ban on the practice unconstitutional.

dailyNews.description: A system that thousands of schools and universities use was offline due to a cyberattack.

dailyNews.description: Iran accuses the US of violating the truce, alleging it targeted an oil tanker and carried out attacks on coastal areas.

dailyNews.title: Iran focus at Trump-Xi summit may delay progress on tariffs, rare earths
dailyNews.description: Thanks to the Iran war, big issues for U.S. businesses like rare earths and supply chains may take a back seat on the agenda for the Trump-Xi summit.

dailyNews.title: Trade Court Rules Trump’s 10% Global Tariff Is Illegal
dailyNews.description: A panel of federal judges found that President Trump could not legally impose the tariff on most imports.

dailyNews.title: Trump Reversed Hormuz Plan After Saudis Denied Airspace Access
dailyNews.description: Saudi Arabia’s refusal of support suggests that President Trump’s unpredictable approach to Iran has strained ties with one of his closest allies in the Middle East.

dailyNews.title: Trade court strikes down a second round of Trump tariffs
dailyNews.description: The Court of International Trade has struck down a second round of global tariffs ordered by President Trump, after his earlier import taxes were outlawed by the U.S. Supreme Court.

dailyNews.title: Mark Hamill deletes image depicting Trump as deceased, posts new photo to 'apologize' after backlash
dailyNews.description: Actor Mark Hamill deleted a photo depicting President Donald Trump as deceased and posted a new image on Bluesky after facing backlash on Thursday.

dailyNews.title: U.S. Debt Hits a Concerning Milestone, and Experts Say Trump’s Policies Could Worsen It
dailyNews.description: The debt is outgrowing the size of America’s economy. The president’s policies could accelerate the country’s fiscal headaches, experts say, unless policymakers intervene.

dailyNews.title: Hantavirus Response Shows How Trump Cuts Have Compromised U.S. Preparedness
dailyNews.description: The Trump administration has slashed funding for infectious disease research and has far fewer employees, including disease detectives, to respond to outbreaks.

dailyNews.title: AAPIs more likely than others to say recent Trump policies negatively impacted them: study
dailyNews.description: According to a new study from the Asian American Foundation, Asian Americans and Pacific Islanders are more likely than other Americans to say that recent federal policy changes have negatively impacted their communities. Norman Chen, its CEO, joins "The Daily Report" with more.

dailyNews.title: Are the Trump administration's efforts to lower drug costs working?
dailyNews.description: President Trump has made cutting drug prices a centerpiece of his second term. He's announced deals with major drug companies and launched TrumpRx, where cash-paying patients could find discounted medicines. But a new analysis finds that the reality is more complicated. CBS News medical correspondent Dr. Céline Gounder has more.

dailyNews.title: Trump calls Iran's attack in Strait of Hormuz a "love tap"
dailyNews.description: The U.S. confirmed that three of its military vessels came under Iranian fire in the Strait of Hormuz on Thursday. According to CENTCOM, no U.S. assets were struck and forces were able to intercept the attack. CBS News national security contributor Sam Vinograd has more.

dailyNews.title: Federal judge finds DOGE's elimination of humanities grants "unlawful"
dailyNews.description: Three of the nation's major scholarly groups challenged the Trump administration's cuts to humanities grants.

dailyNews.title: Examining the strength of Trump's presidential pardoning power
dailyNews.description: CBS News has exclusively learned that Senate and House Democrats are investigating whether President Trump issued pardons and commutations as part of what is described as a "pay-to-play" dynamic. Lawmakers have sent letters to more than a dozen recipients of executive clemency, seeking documents about payment history and communications with lawyers or those close to the president. CBS News legal contributor Jessica Levinson has more.
